title: The empty bench in winter
summary: Describe one public bench in cold weather and notice how people use a shared space differently when it is empty.
topic: A bench in a public place that stays empty in winter
prompt: Write 8–10 short sentences about one bench in a park, street, or square. Say where it is, who sits there in warm weather, and why it is empty in winter. Use present simple, past simple, and adverbs of frequency. Describe what you see and how the empty bench makes you feel.
hintStarters: There is a bench near… and in summer it is always…
In winter the bench is empty because…
When I walk past it, I sometimes…
sampleText: There is a small wooden bench near the bus stop on my street. In summer it is always busy. Old people sit there in the morning and young people sit there at night. They talk and eat ice cream and watch the cars. But in winter the bench is empty. It is too cold and the wood is wet. Last week I saw frost on the bench in the morning. Nobody sat there for hours. The leaves were brown on the ground around it. I walked past it slowly. The street was very quiet. I never sit on the bench in winter, but I look at it every day. It feels strange because in summer it is full of voices. In winter it just waits. I think a bench is a small thing, but it shows the seasons. When the bench is empty, I know the cold is here. When people come back, I know spring is near. I always smile a little when the first person sits down again.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi đi ngang qua nó một cách chậm rãi

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple for habits, past simple for one event
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng hiện tại đơn cho thói quen lặp lại ('Old people sit there', 'I look at it every day') và quá khứ đơn cho một việc đã xảy ra một lần ('Last week I saw frost', 'Nobody sat there'). Hãy phân biệt rõ hai thì này khi tả.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present simple for repeated habits ('Old people sit there', 'I look at it every day') and the past simple for one finished event ('Last week I saw frost', 'Nobody sat there'). Keep the two tenses clearly separate.

sampleTranslationVi: Có một chiếc ghế dài nhỏ bằng gỗ gần trạm xe buýt trên phố nhà tôi. Vào mùa hè nó luôn đông người. Người già ngồi đó vào buổi sáng và người trẻ ngồi đó vào buổi tối. Họ trò chuyện, ăn kem và ngắm xe cộ. Nhưng vào mùa đông chiếc ghế trống không. Trời quá lạnh và gỗ thì ẩm ướt. Tuần trước tôi thấy sương giá trên ghế vào buổi sáng. Không ai ngồi ở đó suốt mấy tiếng. Lá cây màu nâu nằm trên mặt đất quanh nó. Tôi đi ngang qua nó một cách chậm rãi. Con phố rất yên tĩnh. Tôi không bao giờ ngồi trên ghế vào mùa đông, nhưng ngày nào tôi cũng nhìn nó. Cảm giác thật lạ vì vào mùa hè nó đầy ắp tiếng nói. Vào mùa đông nó chỉ chờ đợi. Tôi nghĩ một chiếc ghế là điều nhỏ bé, nhưng nó cho thấy các mùa. Khi chiếc ghế trống không, tôi biết cái lạnh đã về. Khi người ta quay lại, tôi biết mùa xuân đã gần kề. Tôi luôn mỉm cười một chút khi người đầu tiên ngồi xuống trở lại.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural sentence to describe the bench in summer.
options: In summer the bench is always busies.
In summer the bench always busy.
In summer the bench is always busy.
In summer the bench is full of cars and noise machines.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'is always busy' uses 'be' + adverb of frequency + adjective correctly and fits the topic.
mcQuestions.hint: Where does 'always' go with the verb 'be'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses the past simple for one event last week?
options: Last week I seen frost on the bench.
Last week I see frost on the bench.
Last week I was seeing the bench frost on.
Last week I saw frost on the bench.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'saw' is the correct past simple of 'see'.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the past simple of 'see'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best explains why the bench is empty in winter?
options: It is empty because it is too cold and the wood is wet.
It is empty because too cold and wood wet.
It is empty so the cold and the wet wood.
Because empty the bench it is cold and wet wood.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'because' is followed by two full clauses joined with 'and'.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'because', you need a complete clause with subject and verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple form: 'Old people ___ there in the morning.'
options: sits
sit
sitting
sat
mcQuestions.explanation: 'people' is plural, so the verb takes no -s: 'sit'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence sounds most natural and on-topic?
options: When people comes back, I know spring is near.
When people come back, I know spring is nearly.
When people come back, I know spring is near.
When people come back, I know the database is near.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'people come' (no -s, plural) and 'spring is near' are both correct and natural.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the verb after 'people' and the word after 'is'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says you do something every day?
options: I looks at it every day.
I look at it every days.
Every day at it I look.
I look at it every day.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'I look' takes no -s; 'every day' (singular 'day') is the correct phrase.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'but' to show a contrast between the seasons?
options: In summer it is busy, but in winter it is empty.
In summer it is busy, but in winter empty it is.
In summer busy, but winter empty.
But in summer it is busy in winter it is empty.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'but' joins two complete clauses, each with subject + 'is' + adjective.
mcQuestions.hint: Both parts joined by 'but' need a subject and a verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple: 'Nobody ___ there for hours.'
options: sit
sat
sits
sitted
mcQuestions.explanation: 'sat' is the irregular past simple of 'sit'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes the empty bench in the most natural way?
options: In winter it just waiting.
In winter it just wait.
In winter it just waits.
In winter it just waits for the cars to talk.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'it waits' is correct (third-person -s) and the simple image fits the topic.
mcQuestions.hint: The subject 'it' needs which verb ending?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses an adverb of frequency about your feeling?
options: I smile always a little when the first person sits down.
Always a little I smile when sits down the first person.
I always smiling a little when the first person sits down.
I always smile a little when the first person sits down.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'always' goes before the main verb 'smile': 'I always smile'.
mcQuestions.hint: Where does 'always' go with a main verb (not 'be')?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do you think an empty public seat can feel sad or lonely, even though it is just an object that nobody is using right now?
